ai_overview: Viktor's Stakeholder Digest email was unavailable for 06/05/2026 — Microsoft 365 authentication is pending and this digest is built from Slack and Jira sources only. Wednesday 6 May was a highly productive day, dominated by Delivery Cycle 9 release preparation. Chloe Christie posted the full Thursday release manifest in #6amigos — 15 tickets going live including SlotsLaunch Game Grid (GDCU-8900), Brand Carousel (GDCU-8861), Game/Software Provider Names (GDCU-8865), Author Carousel Style (GDCU-8849), Content Layouts (GDCU-8842), and Phone Number Email Confirmation (GDCU-8877). Harden Wallace was the standout performer with 9 Jira tickets across Done, In QA, and In Progress — the highest single-day output in recent sprints. Carlos Blauth, Novica Vukobratovic, Jessica Dordevic, and Ciara Carroll each had 3–4 tickets updated. A major strategic discussion unfolded in a senior stakeholder group DM: Gabriel Cornoiu formally flagged serious timeline risk on the World Cup Predictor game, arguing only 2–2.5 weeks remain, the project is data/backend-heavy, and would require Harden, Kelvin, Carlos, and Eoin simultaneously — recommending pivoting to Cosmo Spins WC reskin, Standard.co.uk, and SEO tasks instead. Dean Ryan provided GA analytics showing April game page traffic at 12k (vs March 27k) after stripping ~13k spam Indonesian users. Shane McGrath and Novica investigated a Cosmo Spins in-game session bug triggered by Google OAuth sign-up. Ciara Carroll confirmed a T&C scrollable/static design solution is in progress. Jessica Dordevic shared a Netlify prototype for the Quiz Hub (GDCU-8903). Miguel Migneco was on pre-planned leave. Vic Dadson remains on medical sick leave.

people.name: Vic Dadson
people.summary: Remains on medical sick leave from 21 April. No Slack or Jira activity on 6 May. Sick note was for a fortnight from April 21, expiring around May 5 — return status not yet confirmed in visible channels. Mike Pett is back in office from today (May 7) after his own leave period.

people.name: Jessica Dordevic
people.summary: Highly active on 6 May across design and DMs. Shared a Netlify prototype for the Quiz Hub (GDCU-8903) with Mike — responsive design covering all considered pages — and discussed which sports to include for the WC Predictor quiz, plus updated the picks component. In a group DM with Ciara and Mike she worked on IUJ Banners (GDCU-8928): addressed mobile/375px button placement constraints, confirmed profile-link logic ('everything is left as it was — both link to the profile'), and marked the design prepared for dev. In #gdc-product-design-pod confirmed she is reviewing Harden's comments on GDCU-8834 at Chloe's request. Jira: GDCU-8301 Done (Author Images/Gemini Style Update), GDCU-8879 Done (Genesis Brand Carousel Card), GDCU-8928 In Product (IUJ Banners), GDCU-8546 In Progress (Tinder Style Offer Match).

people.name: Miguel Migneco
people.summary: Pre-planned day off on 6 May — flagged to Mike in advance on 30 April. No Slack or Jira activity. Last active 29 April in #gdc-product-led-growth demonstrating his Claude Design workflow for the Group Bets widget, citing a 15-minute Claude prototype that would normally take 3+ hours in Figma.

people.name: Ciara Carroll
people.summary: Very active on 6 May across design, DMs, and Jira. Ran a busy thread with Michael Butlin and Mike on Learning Hub imagery (GDCU-8736): iterated colours (chose blue variant, updating remaining two), shared Figma prototype link, and received detailed design feedback from Michael on roulette/blackjack imagery swap and Myth Buster colour. Also confirmed a T&C design solution is in progress ('I have the solution, we discussed it Mike — just needs some updates so I'll sort that now'), with a full prototype planned for both full and partial terms. In a group DM with Jessica, Miguel, and Mike provided design direction against gradients in hero sections ('a clean look with drop shadows, coloured strokes on rounded edge containers, iconography from our icon library'). Jira: GDCU-8736 Done (Learning Hub Imagery), GDCU-8619 In Progress (Oplist Redesign), GDCU-8947 In Product (Cosmo Cash Machine Images), GDCU-8956 To Do (Logo Generator for Claude Design System — ticket created by Chloe).

people.name: Josh Smith
people.summary: Minimal traceable activity on 6 May — no direct Slack posts found. Was a participant in the group DM where Chloe asked Mike about GDCU-8849 sign-off. Gabriel referenced Josh as the owner of the fixture data API provider (the-odds-api.com) in the WC Predictor senior stakeholder discussion. Product Sync meeting took place on May 6 (Chloe shared the agenda to #gdc-product-design-pod). No Jira tickets assigned or updated under Josh on 6 May.

people.name: Chloe Christie
people.summary: Active across multiple channels managing DC9 release coordination on 6 May. In #6amigos at 09:33 posted the Thursday (May 7) release manifest — 9 BAU tickets and 5 high-priority: GDCU-8900 (SlotsLaunch Game Grid), GDCU-8861 (Brand Carousel), GDCU-8865 (Game Provider Names/Sizing), GDCU-8849 (Author Carousel Shortcode), GDCU-8842 (Content Layouts), plus GDCU-8877, GDCU-8834, GDCU-8929, GDCU-8931. In #gdc-product-design-pod: asked Jessica to review Harden's GDCU-8834 comments before release, flagged GDCU-8865 requires a UI button decision before going live, and shared the May 6 product sync agenda. Created ticket GDCU-8956 (Logo Tool for Claude DS) for Ciara. Also in a group DM with Mike and Josh checking approval on GDCU-8849.

people.name: Carlos Blauth
people.summary: Active on Jira with 4 tickets updated on 6 May — no direct Slack messages found. Jira: GDCU-8733 Done (Learning Hub Feedback — initial phase), GDCU-8806 In Product (In-App Age Gate 18+ Verification for FTP app), GDCU-8951 In Progress (Learning Hub Phase 2 Feedback application), GDCU-8936 In Progress (Redesign of Ace Navigation with Global Nav Bar). Parallel development across FTP app, Learning Hub, and Ace projects.

people.name: Eoin McCarthy
people.summary: No Slack activity or Jira ticket updates found on 6 May. Gabriel Cornoiu named Eoin as one of the four developers who would be required to simultaneously deliver the World Cup Predictor game — flagging this as a resource risk. Last confirmed Slack activity was 22 April (DM with Mike on Betting Tips Hub pagination).

people.name: Harden Wallace
people.summary: Exceptional Jira output on 6 May — 9 tickets updated across all states. Done: GDCU-8861 (Brand Carousel Integration — Gabriel confirmed Genesis releasing the component Thursday morning before the weekly release), GDCU-8834 (Cool Tones Update Fixes — Chloe noted in #gdc-product-design-pod that Harden is releasing what he's done tomorrow, with remaining comment-section fixes next week pending UX feedback from Jessica), GDCU-8929 (Sidebar Navigation to Casino/Betting Strategy Articles), GDCU-8849 (New Style on Author Carousel Shortcode), GDCU-8865 (Game/Software Provider Names and Sizing — Chloe flagged a UI button decision needed before release), GDCU-8931 (Strategy pages not appearing in search — fix resolved). In QA: GDCU-8874 (Page Bloat/Cookie — Reduce HTML payload across markets), GDCU-8955 (Cool Tones Phase 2 Fixes). In Progress: GDCU-8810 (Apple/Google Organisation Level Developer Accounts). Six of his Done tickets are included in tomorrow's DC9 release manifest.

people.name: Kelvin Yee
people.summary: Two Jira tickets updated on 6 May: GDCU-8878 In QA (CA/FR Oplist Redesign on Online Casinos Page — a high-priority DC9 feature), GDCU-8900 Done (SlotsLaunch Game Grid Shortcode — included in tomorrow's release). Dean Ryan confirmed in a group DM with Colin and Mike that Kelvin will take the Quick Pick feature implementation on May 7, with Dean providing the business logic.

people.name: Milan Nikolic
people.summary: No Slack or Jira activity on 6 May. No change from previous digest. Last known Slack activity was late March 2026 on the Global Strategy Hub (GDCU-8653) discussion.

people.name: Nebojsa Stanic
people.summary: No Slack or Jira activity on 6 May. No change from previous digest.

people.name: Novica Vukobratovic
people.summary: High Jira activity on 6 May with 3 tickets updated: GDCU-8877 Done (Add Field to Manage Phone Numbers on Email Confirmation Pages in Admin Panel — included in tomorrow's DC9 release, a key delivery for the team), GDCU-8894 In QA (Refactor Scoped Styles on Vue Components), GDCU-8925 In Progress (Implement CWV Changes Recommended by Viktor AI). Also involved in a group DM with Shane McGrath and Mike investigating a Cosmo Spins in-game session bug triggered by Google OAuth sign-up — the sign-up causes a page reload that kicks users out of the game. Shane confirmed that normal email/password sign-up maintains session on mobile.

people.name: Sanja Budic
people.summary: Two Jira tickets updated on 6 May — no direct Slack messages found. GDCU-8907 In QA (Implement Betting Tips Hub Page — a high-priority DC9 feature), GDCU-8899 In Progress (Research: How to Block Restricted VPN Users from Playing FTP Games). Steady progress on both workstreams.

people.name: Robin John
people.summary: No Slack or Jira activity found on 6 May. Last confirmed activity was 30 April when Robin posted QA sign-off for all DC8 release tickets in #gdc-pod — all clear, with a note that some content-related issues needed separate logging. With the DC9 release planned for May 7, QA sign-off activity is likely upcoming today.

people.name: Shane McGrath
people.summary: Low activity on 6 May focused on a Cosmo Spins bug investigation. In a group DM with Mike and Novica, Shane tested the in-game login/sign-up flow — confirmed normal email/password sign-up maintains session on mobile ('Normal sign up with email/password, I remain in the game — checked on mobile'). Identified that the Google OAuth in-game issue is a known problem with a previously discussed but not-yet-implemented fix (open Google login in a modal within the game). Also responded briefly in #gdc-pod to Gabriel's Cosmo Spins memory management workaround suggestion.

people.name: Dean Ryan
people.summary: Active across analytics and product decisions on 6 May. In #gdc-leads-team provided Google Analytics data on game page traffic: March 27k, April 12k, May to date 4k — after removing ~13k spam Indonesian users ('I've removed the spam Indonesian traffic'). Noted April drop was related to tournament changes. Via DM with Mike discussed commercial partnership potential for games. In a group DM with Colin and Mike approved the design for Dean's Quick Pick feature and confirmed Kelvin will implement it on May 7 with Dean providing the business logic. Also expressed frustration about AI-generated content quality in analytics reports ('It's riddled — everything is complimentary — great idea Dean, yes you are right Dean — hard to navigate'). This morning (May 7) asked for clarification on Colin's #core-division-leads matrix request.

people.name: Michael Butlin
people.summary: Active in design reviews and data governance on 6 May. In a group DM with Ciara Carroll and Mike, reviewed Learning Hub imagery designs — praised Ciara's work overall ('Very very interesting! I really like these'), requested purple for the Myth Buster image, flagged the roulette wheel in the Blackjack Strategy Trainer should be replaced with cards/blackjack imagery, and approved the blue colour variant for other assets. In a separate group DM (senior stakeholders incl. Colin, Dean, Gabriel, Mike, Dan), raised a question on Cosmo Cash Machine brand attribute collection — querying whether attributes not displayed in oplists (e.g. BJK Providers) need to be collected at all, and confirming wagering multiplier needs to be retained if it maps to 'Bonus Wagering' on oplists.

people.name: Dan Smullen
people.summary: No Slack messages found on 6 May despite being a participant in the senior stakeholder group DM (Colin, Dean, Michael, Gabriel, Mike, Dan). Last known personal activity was 28 April navigating Claude Design access with Mike. No Jira ticket activity visible.

people.name: Gabriel Cornoiu
people.summary: Engaged in key strategic and technical discussions on 6 May. In the senior stakeholder group DM, raised a detailed risk assessment on the World Cup Predictor game: 'The chance to make it on time for the World Cup is low. This project is data and backend-heavy.' Outlined the full scope of moving parts — pages/widgets, data integrations, login, CRM journeys, points tracking, leaderboard, prizes, legal, and results management. Flagged he would need Harden, Kelvin, Carlos, and Eoin working simultaneously, and warned about matrix data gaps. Referenced the-odds-api.com as Josh's current fixture data provider and requested access. Recommended prioritising Cosmo Spins WC reskin, Standard.co.uk partnership, SEO/internal linking, and Ace Assistant instead. Also in #gdc-pod responded to Shane with a Cosmo Spins memory management workaround (open new tab to let the game tab idle/suspend, then reopen). Brief DM exchange with Mike.

people.name: Diana Thurmann
people.summary: No personal Slack activity on 6 May. The automated n8n iGaming Daily Digest workflow she set up ran successfully at 08:01 BST in #gdc-nonen-market-updates — 72 articles scanned across 6 sources (SBC News, iGamingToday, GamblingNews, iGaming Expert, European Gaming, TheGamblest), covering global news, US, UK, Netherlands, Belgium, Czech Republic, France, Norway, Sweden, Georgia, Brazil, Mexico, Philippines, Australia, Africa, Canada, Chile, Kazakhstan. The automation is running independently without manual intervention.

people.name: Colin Brannigan
people.summary: Active across reporting and product discussions on 6 May. In #gdc-leads-team raised the question of whether there was a CRM push change to games in April vs March after reviewing a traffic report — Dean and Mike responded with GA data. In DMs with Mike discussed the World Cup Predictor project complexity, noting login, CRM journeys, and site integration need proper consideration ('don't think it's as straight forward as that — needs to properly work with the existing site'), asked if HR is aware of something (context not visible), and coordinated on the Dean Quick Pick feature. Asked about technical details being resolved for a specific project (user journey, data storage). Late evening (19:06) noted he is in the office today (May 7). This morning (May 7, 05:23) sent an early message to #core-division-leads requesting all matrix inputs from division leads by COB today.
